meta.title: theo

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: theo
root.mentor_note: theo is one of the oldest roots in the grove, and one of the most direct.

attempt.direction_prompt: Rule by an assembly, or rule in a god’s name?

options.text: In the name of a god

context_sentences: His degree in theology led him first to teaching, and only later to the priesthood.
The seminar on medieval theology drew students from departments that had nothing else in common.
The new constitution rejected theocracy outright, separating the temple from the treasury for the first time.
Under the old theocracy, no law could pass without the high priest’s explicit approval.
The epic’s central chapter describes a theomachy so violent that mountains are said to have split apart.
Ancient vase paintings often depicted scenes from a theomachy, gods hurling weapons across the sky.

construct_options.text: A battle or conflict fought among gods in myth
A formal debate held among religious scholars
A machine built to imitate the power of the gods

context_sentences: He had been a quiet atheist for years before he ever said so at the family dinner table.
The debate paired a theologian with an atheist, and both left admitting the other had a point.

construct_options.text: A person who does not believe in the existence of any god
A person who worships more than one god
A person who studies religion professionally
